---
title: Protein Database Description
subtitle: SpringBloom2021 - Genome-resolved Metaproteomic Dataset
date: 09.04.2025
author: Cyrill Hofer
output:
  rmdformats::readthedown:
    highlight: tango
    self_contained: true
    code_folding: hide
    thumbnails: true
    gallery: false
    fig_width: 15
    df_print: kable
    fig_caption: yes
    use_bookdown: true
    css: custom.css
---

# Data Preparation
- Note: This data does not contain and protein identifications from contaminants
```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, paged.print=FALSE}
# Clear R's Brain
rm(list = ls())

# Set the working directory
setwd("/SynologyDrive/PhD/Projects/SpringBloom2021/DataAnalysis_SpringBloom2021/Supplementary/SpringBloom_Metagnomics/DatabaseOverview/DatabaseDescription/")

# Load the libraries
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(DT)
library(plotly)
library(tidyr)
library(scales)
library(forcats)
library(patchwork)
library(data.table)
library(stringr)

# Load the color palette
source("ColorPalette.R")

# Load the data
dat <- read_csv(file = "ProteinDataAnnotated.csv")
```


```{r}
# 1) Summarise per species ---------------------------------------------------
species_summary <- dat %>%
  group_by(Phylum, Species_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    Proteins = n(),
    Peptides = sum(Nr_Tryptic_Peptides, na.rm = TRUE),
    KEGG_Classified = sum(!is.na(KEGG_ID) & KEGG_ID != "Unclassified"),
    .groups = "drop")

# 2) Compute per‐phylum totals & global maxima --------------------------------
phylum_totals <- species_summary %>%
  group_by(Phylum) %>%
  summarise(total_proteins = sum(Proteins),
            total_peptides = sum(Peptides),
            total_kegg_classified = sum(KEGG_Classified), .groups = "drop")

# global maxima for label placement
global_max_prot <- max(species_summary$Proteins, na.rm = TRUE)
global_max_pep  <- max(species_summary$Peptides, na.rm = TRUE)
global_max_kegg <- max(species_summary$KEGG_Classified, na.rm = TRUE)

# add fixed y‐positions (5% above max)
phylum_totals <- phylum_totals %>%
  mutate(y_prot = global_max_prot * 1.05,
         y_pep  = global_max_pep  * 1.05,
         y_kegg = global_max_kegg * 1.05)

# overall totals for subtitles
overall_proteins <- sum(phylum_totals$total_proteins)
overall_peptides <- sum(phylum_totals$total_peptides)
overall_kegg <- sum(phylum_totals$total_kegg_classified)

# 3) Order Phyla by median Proteins for consistent x‐axis ---------------------
phylum_order <- species_summary %>%
  group_by(Phylum) %>%
  summarise(med = median(Proteins), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(med)) %>%
  pull(Phylum)

species_summary <- species_summary %>%
  mutate(Phylum = factor(Phylum, levels = phylum_order))
phylum_totals <- phylum_totals %>%
  mutate(Phylum = factor(Phylum, levels = phylum_order))

# 4) Color mapping ------------------------------------------------------------
fill_cols <- setNames(ColorPalette[seq_along(phylum_order)], phylum_order)

# 5) Build box+dot plots with fixed‐height totals ----------------------------

# a) Proteins per species
p_prot <- ggplot(species_summary, aes(Phylum, Proteins)) +
   geom_jitter(aes(fill = Phylum), width  = 0.35, height = 0, shape  = 21, color  = "black", size = 2.5, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(fill = Phylum), width = 0.75, outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.3, color = "black") +
  geom_text(data = phylum_totals, aes(x = Phylum, y = y_prot, label = total_proteins), size = 2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_cols, guide = "none") +
  labs(title = paste0("Proteins per species  (total=", overall_proteins, ")"), x = NULL, y = "Proteins [n]") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0.5),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14),
        legend.position = "none",
        plot.margin = margin(5,5,5,5))

# b) Tryptic peptides per species
p_pep <- ggplot(species_summary, aes(Phylum, Peptides)) +
  geom_jitter(aes(fill = Phylum), width  = 0.35, height = 0, shape = 21, color = "black", size = 2.5, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(fill = Phylum), width = 0.75, outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.3, color = "black") +
  geom_text(data = phylum_totals, aes(x = Phylum, y = y_pep, label = total_peptides), size = 2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_cols, guide = "none") +
  labs(title = paste0("Tryptic peptides per species  (total=", overall_peptides, ")"), x = NULL,
    y = "Peptides [n]") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0.5), 
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14),
        legend.position = "none",
        plot.margin = margin(5,5,5,5))

# c) KEGG‐classified proteins per species
p_kegg <- ggplot(species_summary, aes(Phylum, KEGG_Classified)) +
  geom_jitter(aes(fill = Phylum), width  = 0.35, height = 0, shape  = 21, color = "black", size = 2.5, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(fill = Phylum), width = 0.75, outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.3, color = "black") +
  geom_text(data = phylum_totals, aes(x = Phylum, y = y_kegg, label = total_kegg_classified), size = 2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_cols, guide = "none") +
  labs(title = paste0("KEGG classified proteins per species  (total=", overall_kegg, ")"),
       x = "Phylum",
       y = "KEGG IDs [n]") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0.5),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 12),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14),
        legend.position = "none",
        plot.margin = margin(5,5,5,5))

# 6) Combine vertically with overall title -----------------------------------
final_plot <- (p_pep / p_prot / p_kegg) +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1, 1.2)) +
  plot_annotation(title = "LZ-BITS", 
                  theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)))

print(final_plot)

# 7) Export
ggsave("Per_phylum_database_summary.png",
       plot   = final_plot,
       device = png,
       width  = 12,
       height = 7,
       units  = "in",
       dpi    = 300)

```

```{r}
# STEP 1: Generate long-format peptide-level data
pep_long <- dat %>%
  select(Phylum, Protein_ID, Protein_Length, Tryptic_Peptides, Peptide_Length) %>%
  mutate(
    Peptide = str_split(Tryptic_Peptides, ";"), 
    Peptide_Length = str_split(Peptide_Length, ";") %>% lapply(as.integer)
  ) %>%
  unnest(c(Peptide, Peptide_Length)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Peptide_Length))


# Order Phylum factor by mean protein length
phylum_order <- pep_long %>%
  group_by(Phylum) %>%
  summarise(mean_prot = mean(Protein_Length, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_prot)) %>%
  pull(Phylum)

pep_long <- pep_long %>%
  mutate(Phylum = factor(Phylum, levels = phylum_order))

# --- PEPTIDE PLOT ---

# Compute per-phylum counts for labeling
n_peptides <- nrow(pep_long)

phylum_pep_counts <- pep_long %>%
  group_by(Phylum) %>%
  summarise(n_peptides = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(Facet_Label = paste(Phylum, "(n =", n_peptides, ")"))

pep_long_peptides <- pep_long %>%
  left_join(phylum_pep_counts, by = "Phylum") %>%
  mutate(Facet_Label = factor(Facet_Label, levels = phylum_pep_counts$Facet_Label))

# Compute means and label positions
peptide_means <- pep_long_peptides %>%
  group_by(Facet_Label) %>%
  summarise(
    Peptide_Mean = mean(Peptide_Length, na.rm = TRUE),
    
    # Filter out NAs before calling density()
    Max_Y = max(density(Peptide_Length[!is.na(Peptide_Length)])$y),
    
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Label_Y = Max_Y * 1.1,         # Position label slightly above the density peak
    Label_X = Peptide_Mean + 0.3   # Position label slightly to the right of the mean
  )


# Plot
p_pep_len <- ggplot(pep_long_peptides, aes(x = Peptide_Length, fill = Phylum)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.6, adjust = 1.2) +
  geom_vline(
    data = peptide_means,
    aes(xintercept = Peptide_Mean),
    linetype = "dashed", color = "black"
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = peptide_means,
    aes(x = Label_X, y = Label_Y, label = round(Peptide_Mean, 1)),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    hjust = -0.5, angle = 0, size = 2.5
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ Facet_Label, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(6, 31), breaks = seq(6, 31, by = 2)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = ColorPalette) +
  labs(
    title = paste("Peptide length distribution (7–30 aa) — Total N =", n_peptides, "peptides"),
    x = "Peptide length (aa)",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 8) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 8),
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  )

print(p_pep_len)

# --- PROTEIN PLOT ---

# Distinct protein-level dataset
pep_long_proteins <- pep_long %>%
  distinct(Phylum, Protein_ID, Protein_Length)

# Count proteins per phylum
n_proteins <- n_distinct(pep_long_proteins$Protein_ID)

phylum_prot_counts <- pep_long_proteins %>%
  group_by(Phylum) %>%
  summarise(n_proteins = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(Facet_Label = paste(Phylum, "(n =", n_proteins, ")"))

pep_long_proteins <- pep_long_proteins %>%
  left_join(phylum_prot_counts, by = "Phylum") %>%
  mutate(Facet_Label = factor(Facet_Label, levels = phylum_prot_counts$Facet_Label))

# Compute means and label positions
protein_means <- pep_long_proteins %>%
  group_by(Facet_Label) %>%
  summarise(
    Protein_Mean = mean(Protein_Length, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max_Y = max(density(log10(Protein_Length))$y),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Label_Y = Max_Y * 1.1,
    Label_X = Protein_Mean * 1.03  # shift right by 3%
  )

# Plot
p_prot_len <- ggplot(pep_long_proteins, aes(x = Protein_Length, fill = Phylum)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.6, adjust = 1.2) +
  geom_vline(
    data = protein_means,
    aes(xintercept = Protein_Mean),
    linetype = "dashed", color = "black"
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = protein_means,
    aes(x = Label_X, y = Label_Y, label = round(Protein_Mean)),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    hjust = -0.5, angle = 0, size = 2.5
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ Facet_Label, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_x_log10(
    breaks = c(0, 30, 100, 300, 1000, 3000, 10000),
    labels = comma_format()
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = ColorPalette) +
  labs(
    title = paste("Protein length distribution (log10) — Total N =", n_proteins, "proteins"),
    x = "Protein length (aa, log10)",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 8) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 8),
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  )

print(p_prot_len)

# Export
ggsave("Plot_Peptide_LengthDistribution_In_Database.png",
       plot   = p_pep_len,
       device = png,
       height = 9,
       width = 12,
       units  = "in",
       dpi    = 300)

ggsave("Plot_Protein_LengthDistribution_In_Database.png",
       plot   = p_prot_len,
       device = png,
       height = 9,
       width = 12,
       units  = "in",
       dpi    = 300)
```


```{r}
# Summarize data per species
species_summary <- dat %>%
  group_by(Phylum, Species_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    Proteins = n(),
    KEGG_Classified = sum(!is.na(KEGG_ID) & KEGG_ID != "Unclassified"),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Proteins", "KEGG_Classified"), names_to = "Metric", values_to = "Count")

# Order Phyla by median protein counts
phylum_order <- species_summary %>%
  filter(Metric == "Proteins") %>%
  group_by(Phylum) %>%
  summarise(med = median(Count), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(med)) %>%
  pull(Phylum)

species_summary$Phylum <- factor(species_summary$Phylum, levels = phylum_order)

# Set color palette
fill_cols <- setNames(ColorPalette[seq_along(phylum_order)], phylum_order)

# Plot combined boxplot for Proteins and KEGG Classified
final_plot <- ggplot(species_summary, aes(x = Phylum, y = Count, fill = Metric)) +
  geom_boxplot(position = position_dodge(width = 0.75), alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA, color = "black") +
  geom_jitter(position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.2, dodge.width = 0.75), shape = 21, color = "black", size = 2, alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Proteins" = "#1f77b4", "KEGG_Classified" = "#ff7f0e")) +
  labs(title = "Proteins and KEGG Classified per Species by Phylum",
       x = "Phylum",
       y = "Count",
       fill = "Metric") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 12),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12)
  )

# Render plot
print(final_plot)

# Export to PDF
ggsave("Proteins_KEGG_per_Species.png",
       plot = final_plot,
       device = png,
       width = 12, height = 8, units = "in", dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
# Summarize data per species
species_summary <- dat %>%
  group_by(Phylum, Species_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    Proteins = n(),
    KEGG_Classified = sum(!is.na(KEGG_ID) & KEGG_ID != "Unclassified"),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c("Proteins", "KEGG_Classified"), names_to = "Metric", values_to = "Count")

# Order Phyla by median protein counts
phylum_order <- species_summary %>%
  filter(Metric == "Proteins") %>%
  group_by(Phylum) %>%
  summarise(med = median(Count), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(med)) %>%
  pull(Phylum)

species_summary$Phylum <- factor(species_summary$Phylum, levels = phylum_order)

# Create shades of colors for each phylum
fill_cols_proteins <- ColorPalette[seq_along(phylum_order)]
fill_cols_kegg <- scales::alpha(fill_cols_proteins, 0.4)
fill_cols <- c(setNames(fill_cols_proteins, paste(phylum_order, "Proteins", sep="_")),
               setNames(fill_cols_kegg, paste(phylum_order, "KEGG_Classified", sep="_")))

# Combine Phylum and Metric for coloring
species_summary <- species_summary %>%
  mutate(Phylum_Metric = paste(Phylum, Metric, sep="_"))

# Plot combined boxplot for Proteins and KEGG Classified with shaded colors
final_plot <- ggplot(species_summary, aes(x = Phylum, y = Count, fill = Phylum_Metric)) +
  geom_boxplot(position = position_dodge(width = 0.75), alpha = 0.9, outlier.shape = NA, color = "black") +
  geom_jitter(position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.2, dodge.width = 0.75), shape = 21, color = "black", size = 2, alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_cols,
                    labels = rep(c("Proteins", "KEGG Classified"), length(phylum_order)),
                    guide = guide_legend(title = "Metric")) +
  labs(title = "Proteins and KEGG Classified per Species by Phylum",
       x = "Phylum",
       y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 12),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12)
  )

# Render plot
print(final_plot)

# Export to PDF
ggsave("Proteins_KEGG_per_Species.png",
       plot = final_plot,
       device = png,
       width = 12, height = 8, units = "in", dpi = 300)

```

```{r}
# Define your desired order
phylum_order <- c(
  "Actinomycetota", "Bacteroidota", "Pseudomonadota",
  "Verrucomicrobiota", "Planctomycetota", "Chloroflexota",
  "Bdellovibrionota", "Acidobacteriota", "Armatimonadota",
  "Myxococcota", "Nitrospirota", "Thermoproteota",
  "Patescibacteria", "Gemmatimonadota", "Cyanobacteriota",
  "Deinococcota", "Chlamydiota"
)

# Calculate mean proteins and KEGG classified per phylum
phylum_means <- dat %>%
  group_by(Phylum, Species_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    Proteins = n(),
    KEGG_Classified = sum(!is.na(KEGG_ID) & KEGG_ID != "Unclassified"),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  group_by(Phylum) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean_Proteins = mean(Proteins),
    Mean_KEGG = mean(KEGG_Classified),
    KEGG_Percentage = (Mean_KEGG / Mean_Proteins) * 100
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(Mean_Proteins))

phylum_means$Phylum <- factor(phylum_means$Phylum, levels = phylum_means$Phylum)

# Set color palette
fill_cols <- ColorPalette[seq_along(levels(phylum_means$Phylum))]

# Plot mean proteins per species and percentage KEGG classified
final_plot <- ggplot(phylum_means, aes(x = Phylum, y = Mean_Proteins, fill = Phylum)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", color = "black", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_bar(aes(y = Mean_KEGG), stat = "identity", color = "black", alpha = 0.9) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_cols) +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Proteins per Species and KEGG Classification by Phylum",
    x = "Phylum",
    y = "Mean Number of Proteins"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 12),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

# Render plot
print(final_plot)

# Export to PDF
ggsave("Mean_Proteins_KEGG_Percentage.png",
       plot = final_plot,
       device = png,
       width = 12, height = 8, units = "in", dpi = 300)
```


```{r}
# Calculate mean proteins and KEGG classified per phylum
phylum_means <- dat %>%
  group_by(Phylum, Species_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    Proteins = n(),
    KEGG_Classified = sum(!is.na(KEGG_ID) & KEGG_ID != "Unclassified"),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  group_by(Phylum) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean_Proteins    = mean(Proteins),
    Mean_KEGG        = mean(KEGG_Classified),
    KEGG_Percentage  = (Mean_KEGG / Mean_Proteins) * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Force the factor levels to your specified order
phylum_means$Phylum <- factor(phylum_means$Phylum, levels = phylum_order)

# (Re-)order the data frame by that factor so everything plots in order
phylum_means <- phylum_means %>%
  arrange(Phylum)

# Set colour palette matching number of phyla
fill_cols <- ColorPalette[seq_along(phylum_order)]

# Plot mean proteins per species and mean KEGG-classified
final_plot <- ggplot(phylum_means, aes(x = Phylum)) +
  geom_col(aes(y = Mean_Proteins, fill = Phylum),
           color = "black", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_col(aes(y = Mean_KEGG,    fill = Phylum),
           color = "black", alpha = 0.9) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_cols) +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Proteins per Species and KEGG Classification by Phylum",
    x     = "Phylum",
    y     = "Mean Number of Proteins"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title        = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.x       = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 12),
    axis.text.y       = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title        = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.position   = "none"
  )

# Render the plot
print(final_plot)

# Export to PDF
ggsave(
  filename = "Mean_Proteins_KEGG_Percentage.png",
  plot     = final_plot,
  device   = png,
  width    = 12, height = 8, units = "in", dpi = 300
)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# 1. Your desired order:
phylum_order <- c(
  "Actinomycetota", "Bacteroidota", "Pseudomonadota",
  "Verrucomicrobiota", "Planctomycetota", "Chloroflexota",
  "Bdellovibrionota", "Acidobacteriota", "Armatimonadota",
  "Myxococcota", "Nitrospirota", "Thermoproteota",
  "Patescibacteria", "Gemmatimonadota", "Cyanobacteriota",
  "Deinococcota", "Chlamydiota"
)

# 2. Compute your stats (no arranging yet):
phylum_means <- dat %>%
  group_by(Phylum, Species_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    Proteins       = n(),
    KEGG_Classified = sum(!is.na(KEGG_ID) & KEGG_ID != "Unclassified"),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  group_by(Phylum) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean_Proteins   = mean(Proteins),
    Mean_KEGG       = mean(KEGG_Classified),
    KEGG_Percentage = Mean_KEGG/Mean_Proteins*100,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 3. **Enforce** YOUR order:
phylum_means <- phylum_means %>%
  mutate(Phylum = factor(Phylum, levels = phylum_order)) %>%
  arrange(Phylum)   # now in exactly phylum_order

# 4. Build the plot (you can also add scale_x_discrete(limits=phylum_order) if you like):
fill_cols <- ColorPalette[seq_along(phylum_order)]

final_plot <- ggplot(phylum_means, aes(x = Phylum)) +
  geom_col(aes(y = Mean_Proteins, fill = Phylum),
           color = "black", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_col(aes(y = Mean_KEGG,    fill = Phylum),
           color = "black", alpha = 0.9) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_cols) +
  scale_x_discrete(limits = phylum_order) +  # *extra* guardrail
  labs(
    title = "Mean Proteins per Species and KEGG Classification by Phylum",
    x     = "Phylum",
    y     = "Mean Number of Proteins"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.x     = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 12),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

print(final_plot)

```


```{r}
# 1. Your desired order:
phylum_order <- c(
  "Actinomycetota", "Bacteroidota", "Pseudomonadota",
  "Verrucomicrobiota", "Planctomycetota", "Chloroflexota",
  "Bdellovibrionota", "Acidobacteriota", "Armatimonadota",
  "Myxococcota", "Nitrospirota", "Thermoproteota",
  "Patescibacteria", "Gemmatimonadota", "Cyanobacteriota",
  "Deinococcota", "Chlamydiota"
)

# 2. Compute your stats:
phylum_means <- dat %>%
  group_by(Phylum, Species_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    Proteins        = n(),
    KEGG_Classified = sum(!is.na(KEGG_ID) & KEGG_ID != "Unclassified"),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  group_by(Phylum) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean_Proteins   = mean(Proteins),
    Mean_KEGG       = mean(KEGG_Classified),
    KEGG_Percentage = Mean_KEGG / Mean_Proteins * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(Phylum = factor(Phylum, levels = phylum_order)) %>%
  arrange(Phylum)

# 3. Colour palette
fill_cols <- ColorPalette[seq_along(phylum_order)]

# 4. Plot with base text size 16, no main title or x-axis label:
final_plot <- ggplot(phylum_means, aes(x = Phylum)) +
  geom_col(aes(y = Mean_Proteins, fill = Phylum),
           color = "black", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_col(aes(y = Mean_KEGG,    fill = Phylum),
           color = "black", alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_text(
    aes(
      y     = Mean_KEGG / 2,
      label = sprintf("%.1f%%", KEGG_Percentage)
    ),
    color    = "white",
    size     = 4,    # slightly larger for base 16
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_cols) +
  scale_x_discrete(limits = phylum_order) +
  labs(
    title = NULL,    # remove main title
    x     = NULL,    # remove x-axis title
    y     = "Mean Number of Proteins"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_blank(),            # ensure title is gone
    axis.title.x    = element_blank(),            # ensure x-title is gone
    axis.text.x     = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

print(final_plot)

```

```{r}
# 1. Your desired order:
phylum_order <- c(
  "Actinomycetota", "Bacteroidota", "Pseudomonadota",
  "Verrucomicrobiota", "Planctomycetota", "Chloroflexota",
  "Bdellovibrionota", "Acidobacteriota", "Armatimonadota",
  "Myxococcota", "Nitrospirota", "Thermoproteota",
  "Patescibacteria", "Gemmatimonadota", "Cyanobacteriota",
  "Deinococcota", "Chlamydiota"
)

# 2. Compute your stats:
phylum_means <- dat %>%
  group_by(Phylum, Species_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    Proteins        = n(),
    KEGG_Classified = sum(!is.na(KEGG_ID) & KEGG_ID != "Unclassified"),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  group_by(Phylum) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean_Proteins = mean(Proteins),
    Mean_KEGG     = mean(KEGG_Classified),
    .groups       = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(Phylum = factor(Phylum, levels = phylum_order)) %>%
  arrange(Phylum)

# 3. Colour palette
fill_cols <- ColorPalette[seq_along(phylum_order)]

# 4. Plot (base size 16, no titles beyond y-axis):
final_plot <- ggplot(phylum_means, aes(x = Phylum)) +
  geom_col(aes(y = Mean_Proteins, fill = Phylum),
           color = "black", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_col(aes(y = Mean_KEGG,    fill = Phylum),
           color = "black", alpha = 0.9) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_cols) +
  scale_x_discrete(limits = phylum_order) +
  labs(
    title = NULL,
    x     = NULL,
    y     = "Mean Genes Per Species"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x    = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x     = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.minor  = element_blank()
  )

print(final_plot)

# Export to PDF
ggsave(
  filename = "Mean_Proteins_KEGG_Percentage.png",
  plot     = final_plot,
  device   = png,
  width    = 12, height = 2, units = "in", dpi = 300
)
```

```{r}
final_plot <- ggplot(phylum_means, aes(x = Phylum)) +
  geom_col(aes(y = Mean_Proteins, fill = Phylum),
           color = "black", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_col(aes(y = Mean_KEGG,    fill = Phylum),
           color = "black", alpha = 0.9) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_cols) +
  # reverse the discrete x (which becomes the vertical axis after coord_flip)
  scale_x_discrete(limits = rev(phylum_order)) +

  coord_flip() +

  labs(
    x = "Phylum",
    y = "Mean Genes Per Species"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x     = element_text(),
    axis.text.y     = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x    = element_text(),
    axis.title.y    = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position  = "none"
  )

print(final_plot)


# Export to PDF
ggsave(
  filename = "Mean_Proteins_KEGG_Percentage.png",
  plot     = final_plot,
  device   = png,
  width    = 3, height = 10.27, units = "in", dpi = 300
)

```
